data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse resulted from a coordinated insurgency of marginalized kinship groups (Sea Peoples) exploiting the vacuum left by the withdrawal of Hittite military protection.", "mechanism": "Asymmetric warfare where decentralized, mobile warrior bands overwhelmed static palace fortifications that relied on chariot-based defense doctrines.", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city-states"], "excludes": ["Egypt"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Epigraphic evidence proving the Sea Peoples were a unified state actor with a centralized command structure rather than disparate migratory groups.", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "A traditional migration/invasion hypothesis, focusing on the military mismatch between rigid palace armies and flexible irregular forces."}
